reader_first_screen_routes.purpose: Choose the reader-specific second screen after the shared local behavior card has made .microcosm state visible.

reader_selection_card.purpose: Let a cold reader choose one branch from a single screen after the shared local behavior proof.
reader_selection_card.shared_rule: Run the shared behavior card first; branch only after .microcosm state, selected_route_id, and the authority ceiling are visible.

selection_rows.cold_question: Can I trust and run this public repo quickly?
selection_rows.trust_signal: copyable first command, shared behavior proof, and authority ceiling stay visible before receipt drilldown.
selection_rows.stop_when: You can run hello/tour --card and name the release, hosting, and reader-success claims this repo refuses.
selection_rows.anti_overread: A first-screen map is not release readiness, publication approval, or proof of reader success.

selection_rows.cold_question: Can I audit the evidence discipline without trusting product claims?
selection_rows.trust_signal: evidence classes, body-import counts, failure modes, and authority ceilings stay separated.
selection_rows.stop_when: You can name what the public package proves, what it only projects, and what remains warning-only.
selection_rows.anti_overread: A low body-import count is a scoped evidence boundary, not a failure score.

selection_rows.cold_question: Is this real enough to judge the builder quickly?
selection_rows.trust_signal: question-to-command mapping, endpoint parity, and no-reader-success guarantees are explicit.
selection_rows.stop_when: You can point to a runnable local card, a 10-minute route, and the boundaries it refuses to cross.
selection_rows.anti_overread: Legibility is interview signal only, not production readiness or benchmark authority.

selection_rows.cold_question: Can I clone it and inspect useful local state in an hour?
selection_rows.trust_signal: .microcosm refs, selected_route_id, causal-chain refs, and source_files_mutated=false are visible.
selection_rows.stop_when: You can rerun the first card, inspect observe/explain refs, and avoid raw receipt spelunking.
selection_rows.anti_overread: A useful route card is not a claim that every organ is complete.

selection_rows.cold_question: Where should I start if I care about one technical domain or specialty?
selection_rows.trust_signal: specialty-to-organ navigation, evidence class, and authority ceiling stay explicit before domain interpretation.
selection_rows.stop_when: You can map a domain to a specialty route and name the evidence ceiling without claiming domain correctness.
selection_rows.anti_overread: Specialty routing is not domain expertise, expert review, or proof that the domain claim is correct.

selection_rows.cold_question: What should I read first as a Type A agent, and where do I patch if the route misleads me?
selection_rows.trust_signal: agent first-read contract, mechanism-vs-validator status, owner surface, and claim ceiling are visible before mutation.
selection_rows.stop_when: You can name the agent first-read path, distinguish mechanisms from validators or projections, and identify the owner surface to patch without overclaiming source mutation.
selection_rows.anti_overread: Agent entry routing is not source-mutation, release, proof-correctness, or private-root-equivalence authority.

reader_selection_card.authority: reader branch selection only; not release, proof-correctness, production, hiring, or safety-evaluation authority

routes.reader_question: Can I trust and run this public repo quickly?
routes.anti_misread: Do not infer release readiness, hosted publication, or reader-success proof from first-screen legibility.

routes.reader_question: Does the evidence discipline survive scale and boundary pressure?
routes.anti_misread: Treat body-import counts as honesty accounting, not a maturity score or release claim.

routes.reader_question: Is this real, and is the builder worth interviewing?
routes.anti_misread: Do not infer production readiness, hosted launch, or benchmark performance from legibility.

routes.reader_question: Can I clone this and inspect useful local state within an hour?
routes.anti_misread: Do not start from raw receipt trees or the full organ inventory when the local route card is enough.

routes.reader_question: Where should I start if I care about one technical domain or specialty?
routes.anti_misread: Do not infer domain expertise, expert review, or domain correctness from specialty routing.

routes.reader_question: What should I read first as a Type A agent, and where do I patch if the route misleads me?
routes.anti_misread: Do not infer source mutation authority, release readiness, proof correctness, or private-root equivalence from agent entry routing.

reader_first_screen_routes.authority: reader routing only; not release, provider, proof-correctness, production, hiring, or safety-evaluation authority

local_first_screen_route.entry_role: bring-a-folder behavioral first contact before validation-suite drilldowns

state_inspection.reader_action: Inspect the local .microcosm files named by state_inspection before doctrine or receipt drilldowns.

local_first_screen_route.selected_route_id_source: plectis tour --card <project>::selected_route_id or plectis tour <project>::selected_route_id or plectis tour <project>::first_screen.selected_route_id or plectis compile <project>::selected_route_id
local_first_screen_route.route_selection_rule: Use the selected_route_id emitted by tour --card, tour, or compile. readme_onboarding_route is a generated route only when the project has a README. Empty or non-README folders can select missing_tests_route, including missing_tests_route when tests are absent.
local_first_screen_route.authority: project-local behavioral route only, not release, provider, source mutation, production, or proof-correctness authority

concept_mechanism_entry_route.entry_role: agent-facing doctrine population and refinement route after the shared first-screen card

standards.reader_rule: Concept rows are typed vocabulary boundaries with source refs, relationships, payload shape, omission receipts, and anti-claims.
Mechanism rows are reusable state, proof, routing, or doctrine transformations with validator attachment.

population_loop.reader_rule: Use the first-screen doctrine frame only as the door; choose one specimen, inspect its concept/mechanism binding, run or cite its validator, then refine the owning standard, pressure row, fixture, or WorkItem residual and record an activation receipt.
sequence: run the specimen validation command or inspect the named receipt
write an activation_receipts row that binds pressure -> specimen -> concept role -> mechanism role -> residual disposition
compile projection_consumers only from population_specimens and activation_receipts when a frontend, HUD, or read-model consumer resumes
refine the owner surface or capture a residual when the specimen cannot carry the pressure
population_loop.build_new_threshold: do not create a parallel concept index unless no existing standard, pressure row, atlas route, diagnostic bundle, fixture manifest, or WorkItem lane can carry the specimen without distortion

concept_binding.concept_role: doctrine handles as typed mistake-prevention boundaries
concept_binding.relationship_shape: first-screen handle -> standard floor -> public pressure row
concept_binding.anti_glossary_rule: CONCEPTS must expose source refs, relationships, payload shape, and public-safe boundary, not a glossary or label list

mechanism_binding.mechanism_role: first-screen projection that routes a reader into runnable validation surfaces
mechanism_binding.transformation_shape: project substrate -> doctrine_effect_frame -> standards and specimen route refs
mechanism_binding.state_or_proof_effect: runtime first-screen output emits CONCEPTS and MECHANISMS before standards-tree search is required
mechanism_binding.anti_feature_prose_rule: MECHANISMS must name a transformation and validator lane, not feature prose or a feature description

anti_claims: This specimen proves entry routing and shape, not doctrine completeness.
This specimen does not authorize release or private-data equivalence.

principle_edge_justifications.P-12: The concept handle only earns authority through the concept/mechanism entry contract, standard floor, validator refs, anti-claims, and payload shape.
principle_edge_justifications.P-15: The first-screen doctrine frame is an entry projection that exposes source-bound handles without upgrading them into source authority.

mechanism_edge_justifications.mechanism.cold_reader_route_map.validate: The accepted cold-reader route-map organ consumes the first-screen card as a handoff and validates the first-run route contract that sends readers to command, public-doc, receipt, and authority-ceiling backed validation surfaces.

axiom_edge_justifications.AX-11: The specimen distinguishes entry prose from executable concept authority by routing through standards and validators.
axiom_edge_justifications.AX-12: The concept handle applies the same anti-claim and authority-boundary floor to Microcosm doctrine handles themselves.

concept_binding.concept_role: standard as public-safe schema contract with explicit authority ceiling
concept_binding.relationship_shape: standard row -> paper module slug -> receipt expectations -> negative cases
concept_binding.anti_glossary_rule: concept population must carry required fields and anti-claims, not glossary or prose-only definitions

mechanism_binding.mechanism_role: validator turns standard rows and fixtures into pass/block receipts
mechanism_binding.transformation_shape: standards bundle -> executable grammar validator -> receipt paths and error codes
mechanism_binding.state_or_proof_effect: invalid standards are blocked by negative cases such as missing teleology, missing receipt expectations, or prose-only authority
mechanism_binding.anti_feature_prose_rule: mechanism population must expose validator attachment and receipt output, not feature prose

anti_claims: This specimen validates public standard shape only.
This specimen does not prove macro doctrine completeness.

principle_edge_justifications.P-8: The executable grammar specimen turns invalid or prose-only doctrine rows into typed blocking reasons instead of green status.
principle_edge_justifications.P-12: The specimen embodies executable doctrine authority through required fields, receipt obligations, anti-claims, and validator coverage.
principle_edge_justifications.P-15: The grammar validator keeps generated standards and paper-module projections below the source grammar and fixture evidence.

mechanism_edge_justifications.mechanism.executable_doctrine_grammar.va: The named mechanism source row validates the exported standards bundle and executable grammar metabolism bundle for this specimen.

axiom_edge_justifications.AX-7: Invalid standard or paper-module rows become typed validation failures rather than inadmissible pass claims.
axiom_edge_justifications.AX-11: The specimen is the public executable grammar lane for doctrine authority.
axiom_edge_justifications.AX-12: The grammar floor applies to Microcosm doctrine artifacts about Microcosm, not only external claims.

concept_binding.relationship_shape: organ id -> standard id -> validator id -> receipt ref
concept_binding.anti_glossary_rule: coverage concepts must state authority ceiling and public/private boundary, not glossary coverage labels

mechanism_binding.mechanism_role: diagnostic bundle checks accepted organs against standards, runtime contracts, and real receipts
mechanism_binding.transformation_shape: exported diagnostics bundle -> coverage projection -> pass/block receipt
mechanism_binding.state_or_proof_effect: accepted_organ_count and covered_organ_ids are validated without becoming release authority
mechanism_binding.anti_feature_prose_rule: diagnostics mechanism must name checked mappings and receipt lane, not feature prose

anti_claims: This specimen is coverage projection, not registry source authority.
This specimen does not authorize release, provider calls, or whole-system correctness.

principle_edge_justifications.P-7: The diagnostics specimen exposes covered organ ids and remaining gaps without treating unmapped space as complete.
principle_edge_justifications.P-13: The specimen applies the same standard, runtime, receipt, anti-claim, and authority floors to Microcosm's own organ-standard coverage.
principle_edge_justifications.P-15: The diagnostics bundle is explicitly projection-only evidence and cannot become standards_registry source authority.

mechanism_edge_justifications.mechanism.standards_meta_diagnostics.val: The named mechanism source row validates accepted-organ standards inventory, runtime contracts, receipt refs, and authority ceilings for this diagnostic specimen.

axiom_edge_justifications.AX-6: Coverage diagnostics remain closed-world only over the declared accepted-organ/standard domain.
axiom_edge_justifications.AX-11: Projection-only diagnostics require schema, receipt, and anti-claim obligations before carrying authority.
axiom_edge_justifications.AX-12: Microcosm's own coverage diagnostics obey the same accountability floor as any external artifact.

concept_binding.concept_role: local pressure rows as typed candidates for owner-surface refinement
concept_binding.relationship_shape: lesson -> pressure shape -> owner surface -> result status
concept_binding.anti_glossary_rule: population pressure must resolve to an owner surface, residual, or nothing_to_refine receipt, not glossary advice

mechanism_binding.mechanism_role: recursive self-improvement loop that senses pressure, classifies, mutates or captures, validates, and publishes reentry
mechanism_binding.transformation_shape: local lessons -> owner surface decisions -> validation and closeout receipt
mechanism_binding.state_or_proof_effect: status_counts distinguish refined_existing_surface, workitem_captured, and nothing_to_refine
mechanism_binding.anti_feature_prose_rule: refinement mechanism must produce result states and reentry conditions, not feature prose or advice-only prose

anti_claims: This specimen demonstrates local refinement, not global doctrine promotion.
This specimen does not authorize hand-editing doctrine nodes or exporting raw voice.

principle_edge_justifications.P-8: The refinement loop must produce a typed refined/captured/nothing_to_refine result instead of inadmissible advice-only prose.
principle_edge_justifications.P-13: Local-to-general refinement of Microcosm artifacts keeps the same evidence, anti-claim, and refusal floors it asks other artifacts to satisfy.
principle_edge_justifications.P-16: The specimen routes mutation or capture through owner surfaces, validation, and closeout receipts rather than ambient edit authority.

mechanism_edge_justifications.mechanism.voice_to_doctrine_self_improve: The named mechanism source row validates the public-safe lesson propagation loop, owner-surface checks, result states, and reentry receipts for this specimen.

axiom_edge_justifications.AX-3: The refinement loop requires dereferenced owner-surface and policy authority instead of possession or advice-only wording.
axiom_edge_justifications.AX-7: Each local pressure row totalizes as refined, captured, nothing_to_refine, or a typed residual with evidence.
axiom_edge_justifications.AX-12: Self-improvement artifacts are held to the same admissibility relation as external claims.

concept_binding.concept_role: agent reliability and safety validators as bounded public claim ceilings
concept_binding.relationship_shape: accepted organ family -> runtime mechanism -> public safety/evaluation receipt -> claim ceiling
concept_binding.anti_glossary_rule: Family concept population must remain source-bound to organ_atlas family rows, not glossary labels or specialty prose.

mechanism_binding.mechanism_role: family runtime validators that turn agent safety, evaluation, red-team, and policy traces into bounded pass/block receipts
mechanism_binding.transformation_shape: fixture or exported public replay bundle -> family validator runtime -> receipt with accepted/rejected rows and anti-claims
mechanism_binding.state_or_proof_effect: safety and reliability claims remain bounded to named public fixtures, validators, and receipt rows instead of global agent capability claims
mechanism_binding.anti_feature_prose_rule: Family mechanism population must name validator and receipt effects, not feature prose or family marketing copy.

anti_claims: This family concept is a source-bound grouping of accepted Microcosm validators, not a claim that the agents are safe in the world.
The concept does not upgrade negative-case coverage into propagation proof or release readiness.

principle_edge_justifications.P-2: The family claim ceiling is lowered to the checker or receipt strength named by its source rows.
principle_edge_justifications.P-3: The family relies on small validator and receipt surfaces rather than broad prose claims.
principle_edge_justifications.P-6: Family status remains fail-closed when required receipts, paths, or mappings are absent.
principle_edge_justifications.P-8: Invalid or incomplete family evidence must become typed refusal/blocking status.

concept_binding.concept_role: source import and projection drift controls as provenance-preserving boundaries
concept_binding.relationship_shape: import/drift organ family -> source-module or projection mechanism -> digest/anchor/receipt boundary
concept_binding.anti_glossary_rule: Family concept population must remain source-bound to organ_atlas family rows, not glossary labels or specialty prose.

mechanism_binding.mechanism_role: family validators that preserve copied source-module digests, projection drift checks, and public-safe import receipts
mechanism_binding.transformation_shape: macro source or projection bundle -> import/drift validator -> digest-bound public receipt
mechanism_binding.state_or_proof_effect: imported material remains tied to source anchors and drift receipts instead of becoming ambient doctrine authority
mechanism_binding.anti_feature_prose_rule: Family mechanism population must name validator and receipt effects, not feature prose or family marketing copy.

omitted: private macro bodies not admitted to public receipts

anti_claims: This family concept does not flip legacy or generated projections into source authority.
Digest parity and drift checks are bounded import evidence, not whole-system correctness.

principle_edge_justifications.P-1: The family concept requires recomputed validator or projection evidence rather than copying generated status as authority.
principle_edge_justifications.P-9: Family edges carry provenance through source rows, code loci, and receipt refs.
principle_edge_justifications.P-11: Drift and freshness facts remain bound to refresh routes and named receipts.
principle_edge_justifications.P-15: Generated projections of the family stay below the source registries and receipts.

mechanism_binding.state_or_proof_effect: research claims remain scoped to public replay evidence and known gaps instead of becoming external scientific truth claims
mechanism_binding.anti_feature_prose_rule: Family mechanism population must name validator and receipt effects, not feature prose or family marketing copy.

omitted: financial advice or live market claims

anti_claims: This family concept is not external scientific validation, financial advice, or benchmark superiority evidence.
Replay receipts expose bounded evidence and gaps, not full replication certainty.

principle_edge_justifications.P-15: Generated projections of the family stay below the source registries and receipts.

concept_binding.concept_role: work landing and continuity controls as transaction-scoped coordination evidence
concept_binding.relationship_shape: work/continuity organ family -> control mechanism -> transaction or continuity receipt -> scoped closeout claim
concept_binding.payload_shape_ref: core/organ_atlas.json.family=work_landing_and_continuity plus Work Ledger and mission transaction refs
concept_binding.anti_glossary_rule: Family concept population must remain source-bound to organ_atlas family rows, not glossary labels or specialty prose.

mechanism_binding.mechanism_role: family validators that bind work landing, bridge continuity, recency coalescing, concurrency, and transaction closeout to receipts
mechanism_binding.transformation_shape: work-control fixture or exported bundle -> coordination validator -> scoped transaction/continuity receipt
mechanism_binding.state_or_proof_effect: work progress claims stay tied to owned paths, receipts, and transaction scope rather than chat status or generated projections
mechanism_binding.anti_feature_prose_rule: Family mechanism population must name validator and receipt effects, not feature prose or family marketing copy.

anti_claims: This family concept does not prove publication readiness, clean branch state, or external delivery.
Coordination receipts do not authorize unrelated path mutation or broad staging.

principle_edge_justifications.P-9: Family edges carry provenance through source rows, code loci, and receipt refs.
principle_edge_justifications.P-10: Work and continuity effects require compensation or explicit transaction scope.
principle_edge_justifications.P-16: Authority is scoped to the transaction or family source row that names the relation.

activation_receipts.pressure_label: Concept index frontend view-compiler substrate residual

concept_binding.concept_role: frontend view contract as a typed semantic commitment, not an independent concept inventory
concept_binding.relationship_shape: WorkItem pressure -> specimen-backed population route -> projection consumer contract
concept_binding.anti_glossary_rule: the frontend concept-index pressure must resolve to a projection contract over specimen bindings, not glossary browsing or a standalone term list

mechanism_binding.mechanism_role: validator gate that requires any frontend compiler to consume population_specimens and preserve validator, receipt, omission, and anti-claim fields
mechanism_binding.transformation_shape: population_specimens -> projection/view compiler contract -> receipt-preserving frontend or renderer surface
mechanism_binding.state_or_proof_effect: activation validator blocks pressure receipts that lack selected specimen, residual disposition, reentry condition, validator refs, anti-claims, or authority boundary
mechanism_binding.anti_feature_prose_rule: the mechanism is a proof-preserving projection gate, not feature prose, a frontend feature description, or a future UI promise

activation_receipts.reentry_condition: resume this residual only as a frontend projection/compiler that consumes atlas/entry_packet.json::concept_mechanism_entry_route.population_specimens and preserves concept_binding, mechanism_binding, validator_refs, receipt_refs, omission_receipt, anti_claims, and authority_boundary fields

omitted: frontend compiler implementation until a compiler consumes the specimen route

anti_claims: This receipt redirects the residual; it does not implement a frontend concept index.
This receipt does not authorize an independent concept inventory, release readiness, provider calls, or private-data equivalence.

projection_consumers.consumer_role: frontend, HUD, and view-compiler projection over concept/mechanism specimens and activation receipts

consumer_receipt.reentry_condition: frontend or HUD work may resume only by consuming this read model or the source concept_mechanism_entry_route; do not author an independent concept inventory

projection_consumers.reentry_condition: frontend or HUD work may resume only by consuming this read model or the source concept_mechanism_entry_route; do not author an independent concept inventory
anti_claims: This consumer is a read model, not a concept registry.
This consumer does not complete frontend product work, authorize release, or replace the source concept/mechanism route.

cold_clone_probe_route.entry_role: validation suite after local first-screen behavior is visible
cold_clone_probe_route.tracked_refresh_rule: Pass --emit only when refreshing an owned tracked receipt on purpose.
cold_clone_probe_route.authority: validator suite receipt, not the first project-local route

python_navigation_route.selection_rule: Start at file_card for Python orientation, then drill to symbol_capsule, graph_context, or source_span only when the task requires exact proof.
python_navigation_route.authority: project-local route pointer, not source authority, release authority, or static-analysis authority

route_explanation_chain_route.full_proof_status_rule: Treat explain as a full first-screen causal-chain proof only after the shared tour card has materialized .microcosm state and the selected-route work transaction; direct explain before project state/work exists can be a partial drilldown.
route_explanation_chain_route.entry_role: first-screen causal-chain proof for how a selected route binds catalog evidence, pattern rows, standard pressure, work transaction state, events, and evidence refs; use plectis observe --card <project> for the compact route/work/event/evidence/graph lens before the expanded explain drilldown
route_explanation_chain_route.authority: project-local causal-chain explanation only, not source authority, release authority, provider authority, live Task Ledger mutation authority, or global doctrine-promotion authority

tour_front_door_status_route.entry_role: cold-reader status rule that separates required first-screen blockers from authority/intake warning drilldowns
tour_front_door_status_route.top_level_status_rule: tour passes only when front_door_status.status equals pass and front_door_status.blocking_surface_ids is empty; authority and intake may still appear in drilldown_blocked_surface_ids as warning drilldowns
tour_front_door_status_route.reader_action: If status is pass and blocking_surface_ids is empty, continue the first-screen path and inspect authority/intake as bounded warnings; if status is blocked or blocking_surface_ids is non-empty, repair the named required surface before claiming the cold path is green.
tour_front_door_status_route.authority: tour status routing only, not source authority, release authority, provider authority, proof authority, or permission to hide blocked drilldowns

status_before_tour_recovery_route.entry_role: honest blocked-status lens when a cold reader asks for status before .microcosm state exists; it must expose the missing state and the recovery command instead of hiding the blocker
status_before_tour_recovery_route.authority: recovery route only, not a waiver for missing project state, release authority, provider authority, source mutation authority, production authority, or proof-correctness authority

status_and_workingness_route.entry_role: cold-reader honesty lens after tour status and before receipt drilldown
status_and_workingness_route.reader_action: Use the status card for selected project route, compact route/work/event/evidence lineage, proof-lab route ref, observatory route ref, source-open body-import counts, and workingness counts; use workingness for per-organ failure envelopes. Treat missing standards and failure modes as bounded debt, not as a score, maturity claim, release gate, or proof authority.
status_and_workingness_route.authority: compressed status and workingness route only, not source authority, release authority, provider authority, proof authority, maturity scoring, or source mutation authority

observatory_route.bounded_validation_rule: Use bounded_validation_command for first-screen route smokes; use command for an interactive browser session.
observatory_route.entry_role: local browser/read-model over the same .microcosm state, route, work, evidence, graph, proof, and status surfaces emitted by the CLI
observatory_route.authority: local observatory/read-model route only, not hosting authority, release authority, provider authority, source authority, source mutation authority, or proof-correctness authority

proof_lab_route.entry_role: cold-reader proof-lab behavior before raw receipt browsing
proof_lab_route.authority: receipt-backed verifier-lab route projection, not theorem proof authority, provider authority, release authority, or source mutation authority

pattern_route_readiness_route.entry_role: selector admission gate before mined pattern rows can route into public organs
pattern_route_readiness_route.authority: selector-readiness validation only, not standalone pattern-leaf authority, source authority, release authority, or publication authority

doctrine_navigation_route.selection_rule: Use local_first_screen_route when the reader asks what to run first with a brought folder; use proof_lab_route when the reader asks what proof-lab behavior is runnable; use status_and_workingness_route when the reader asks whether the first screen is honest about gaps; use observatory_route when the reader asks how the local browser/read-model ties status, route, work, evidence, graph, and proof surfaces together; use python_navigation_route when the reader asks how a brought project becomes .microcosm state; use route_explanation_chain_route or plectis observe --card <project> when the reader asks why a selected route exists or how route, work, events, graph, and evidence connect; use full plectis observe <project> only when event rows are needed; use standards and paper modules as drilldowns after the behavior is visible.

doctrine_lattice_route.entry_role: source-open cold-reader doctrine lattice after behavior is visible
doctrine_lattice_route.selection_rule: Start from proof_lab_route for visible behavior; use observatory_route to inspect the local read-model over status, route, work, evidence, graph, and proof surfaces; use plectis observe --card <project> and route_explanation_chain_route before asking readers to infer the causal chain from receipts; use full plectis observe <project> only when event rows are needed; use pattern_route_readiness_route before treating imported pattern rows as selectable; then use this lattice route to choose explicit principle, candidate-axiom, concept, mechanism, standard, paper-module, and Atlas drilldowns. Do not treat candidate axioms as active law.
doctrine_lattice_route.authority: navigation lattice only, not source authority, release authority, proof authority, doctrine-promotion authority, or candidate-axiom promotion authority

authority_ceiling: entry packet routes public Microcosm navigation; it is not source, release, provider, proof, or mutation authority
anti_claim: This packet is a route pointer, not a source authority or public release surface.
